column1: Luis Arraez stands out as the top pick, riding a strong track record of consistency with hits in over 76% of games and a favorable platoon matchup on the road. Spencer Horwitz also benefits from a soft matchup and park boost, but his recent hit rate lags behind. Jeremy Pena offers similar reliability atop Houston’s lineup, while Bryan Reynolds and Ryan O’Hearn are viable alternatives in a hitter-friendly environment. Arraez’s steady approach and matchup edge make him the most reliable option.
